name: Chiles in Walnut Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 Jalapeno Chile, seeded and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------------WALNUT SAUCE--------------------------------
1 cup Walnuts, ground
1 cup Dairy Sour Cream
1/2 cup Chicken Broth
1. Roast and peel the Pablano Chiles (see note).
2. Use an all-purpose cooking apple that is unpeeled and sliced.
3. Seed and finely chop the Jalapeno Chile.
4. Cut a lengthwise slit down one side of each chile. Carefully remove the seeds and membranes and set aside.
5. Cook and stir ground beef and onion in a 10-inch skillet until the beef is brown. Drain excess fat.
6. Stir in remaining ingredients except eggs, flour, oil, Walnut Sauce, Pomegranate seeds, and cilantro. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
7. Fill chiles with beef mixture. Cover and refrigerate for an hour.
8. Beat egg whites in a large bowl until stiff. Beat egg yolks and fold them into the egg whites.
9. Coat filled chiles with flour; dip into the egg mixture.
10. Heat vegetable oil in an 8-inch skillet until hot (about 350°F).
11. Cook chiles, one at a time, in the oil, turning once, until puffy and golden, about 2 minutes on each side. Drain on paper towels.
12. Place chiles on a wire rack or cooling sheet and keep warm in an oven set to 200°F.
13. Prepare Walnut Sauce and spoon over the chiles. Sprinkle with Pomegranate seeds and cilantro.
14. Serve hot.
